# AI + Three.js: Building 3D Scenes Without Writing a Single Line of Code

> I don't know Three.js. This entire 3D scene was built by talking to AI.

## What This Is

A 3D floor plan of a smart office space, built entirely through AI collaboration. Features:

- 🏢 Building structure (walls, zones, flooring)
- 💡 Device markers (sensors, workstations, smart lights)
- 🎯 Interactive controls (rotate, zoom, click-to-focus)
- 📊 Real-time device status panels
- ✨ Animated pulse rings and floating labels

**100% of the code was generated by AI. I (a PM with no frontend background) only described what I wanted.**

## How It Was Built

```
PM: "I want a 3D office floor plan, top-down view.
     External walls, internal partitions, different zones.
     Device markers in each zone with pulse animations.
     Left panel listing all devices, clickable to focus."

        ↓

AI: [Generates complete Three.js scene]

        ↓

PM: "Lighting is too dim. Add ambient light.
     Change floor to light gray. Add CSS labels to markers."

        ↓

AI: [Adjusts render settings]

        ↓

Done ✅
```

## The PM + AI Collaboration Method

### 1. Describe space in natural language, not coordinates

❌ Bad: "Put a cube at position (12.5, 0, 8.3)"
✅ Good: "Put a blue desk marker in the northwest corner of the open office area, keep some distance from the wall"

Let AI handle the math.

### 2. Layer your descriptions

```
Round 1: Building structure (floor, walls, zones)
Round 2: Device markers (type, color, position)
Round 3: Interaction (orbit, click, side panels)
Round 4: Visual polish (lighting, shadows, animations)
```

Validate each round before moving to the next.

### 3. A sketch beats a thousand words

Draw a rough floor plan on a whiteboard, screenshot it, send it to AI, and say "follow this layout." 10x more efficient than describing everything in text.

## Why This Matters for PMs

In interviews, when someone asks "Can you actually collaborate with engineers?", open this page:

> "I built this 3D visualization by collaborating with AI.
>  I don't know Three.js, but I can describe requirements
>  precisely enough for AI (or an engineer) to implement correctly.
>  That's exactly how I work with technical teams —
>  I define what needs to be built, they figure out how."

## Tech Stack

- Three.js r0.160 (CDN, no build step)
- OrbitControls (rotate / zoom / pan)
- CSS2DRenderer (HTML label overlays)
- Pure frontend — open in browser, no server needed

## Try It

Open `index.html` in any browser. Click devices to focus. Drag to rotate. Scroll to zoom.
